orientation: Practise recurring decimals and exact fractions using the same structure from the learn lesson.

questions.question: Convert 0.333... to a fraction
questions.hint: Use the same single-digit recurring method.

questions.question: Convert 0.121212... to a fraction
questions.hint: Use x and 100x for two recurring digits.

questions.question: Convert 0.4666... to a fraction
questions.hint: Use 10x and 100x to remove the recurring part.

questions.question: Show that 0.818181... = 9/11
questions.hint: Use two-digit recurring method and simplify.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using the wrong power of 10 for the recurring block length.
common_mistakes.correction: Use 10 for one recurring digit, 100 for two, and so on.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not aligning recurring parts before subtraction.
common_mistakes.correction: Write equations so recurring tails match before subtracting.
Reduce the resulting fraction to lowest terms.

summary: Set the recurring decimal equal to x.
Use powers of 10 to align repeating parts.
Subtract, solve for x, then simplify.
